      Very High Confidence
      Last updated 4 Apr 20265.5K Salaries submitted
      The “Most Likely Range” reflects values within the 25th and 75th percentile of all pay data available for this role. Glassdoor salaries are powered by our proprietary machine learning model, which utilises salaries collected from our users and the latest government data to make pay predictions.
      How do we calculate pay?
      How accurate is an average base pay range of £43K-£70K/yr?

      Discuss Data Scientist pay anonymously

      773K

      Salary Negotiations

      Data Scientist

      2w

      I am a lead data scientist with IC role based in Cardiff. I recently moved closer to London location and asked my manager to allign me to london location so that I could get London based salary which he has agreed to work with. My question is, given this is mostly hybrid role with fewer office attendance requirement and given I have initiated the request, what kind of hike could I expect. My current salary is 68K, could I potentially negotiate?

      1
      1 Comment

      Nurse Practitioner 1

      2w

      It might be worth asking your boss if there are other London employees and how much more they are making. That way you have a reasonable benchmark to go off of. You could also do some local market research and see what people are making in London versus Cardiff for the same positions.

            Glassdoor Economist Pay Overview

            What is the pay outlook for Research & Science?
            Jobs in research and science offer pay well above average and are expected to continue to grow in the future. Increasing reliance on data and technology means that demand -- and with it, the pay -- for STEM workers is increasing. However, jobs in these fields often require graduate education and/or specialised training. While domain-specific expertise gives workers in these fields access to very specialised jobs, many other types of employers also hire these employees for their data and technical skills.Show more

            Show more

            Frequently asked questions about Data Scientist salaries

            How much does a Data Scientist in London make?
            The average salary for a Data Scientist is £61,096 per year or £29 per hour in London, which is 12% higher than the national average. Top earners have reported making up to £111,381 (90th percentile). However, the typical pay range in London is between £46,370 (25th percentile) and £82,493 (75th percentile) annually. Salary estimates are based on 5508 salaries submitted anonymously to Glassdoor by Data Scientist employees in London as of April 2026.
            What is the highest salary for a Data Scientist in London?
            The highest salary for a Data Scientist in London is £82,493 per year, £6,874 per month or £40 per hour.
            What is the lowest salary for a Data Scientist in London?
            The lowest salary for a Data Scientist in London is £46,370 per year, £3,864 per month or £22 per hour.
            What is the salary trajectory of a Data Scientist?
            The salary trajectory of a Data Scientist ranges between locations and employers. The salary starts at £53,119 per year (estimate) and goes up to £53,119 per year (estimate) for the highest level of seniority.
            What is the salary trend for Data Scientists?
            As demand for their services has grown over the past decade, data scientist salaries have increased rapidly. They are likely to continue growing as the digitalisation of the economy continues. Junior data scientists will start at an average salary slightly above the national average, but within a few years of experience can expect to earn well above average. Those in leadership or chief scientist roles will earn the highest salaries.
            How To Make More Money as a Data Scientist
            If you want to make more money as a data scientist, you’ll want to be at the cutting edge of the latest systems and technologies. Make sure you're confident and experienced using statistical computer languages such as R, Python, and SQL. Continuously improve your programming skills, statistical analysis abilities, and knowledge of machine learning algorithms. Consider taking advanced courses, attending workshops, and participating in online learning platforms. Increasing your expertise can make you more valuable and potentially lead to higher-paying opportunities.
            Consider specializing in a specific industry or domain where data science is in high demand, such as finance, healthcare, marketing, or e-commerce. This can open doors to more lucrative opportunities.
            How To Negotiate a Higher Salary as a Data Scientist
            If you're looking to increase your salary in your current role, be ready to explain in detail how your performance has been beneficial to the company and how your roles and responsibilities have expanded. Outline the ways your value to the company has increased over time, being as specific as possible. If you've been offered a data scientist job, highlight how your competencies, education, and experience in the field would make you an asset to the team. In both cases, it's helpful to look into data scientist salaries in your area, so you can get a sound idea of where to start.
            Does a Data Scientist make good money in London, UK?
            About 72.17% of Data Scientist professionals feel satisfied with their salary, according to anonymous Glassdoor ratings. An additional £6,109 in potential pay per year, among other factors, can qualify the annual pay of a Data Scientist in London, UK as a good salary.
            How much does a Data Scientist make over time in London, UK?
            Data Scientist professionals in London, UK have a wide total pay range, between £36,686 and £111,381 depending on experience, with an estimated total pay of £61,096 following the average career path of a Data Scientist.
            Salaries >Data Scientist >London
